info.name: GraphOne Backend API
info.description:
# GraphOne Backend API

GraphOne is a startup and tech ecosystem intelligence platform that aggregates data on companies, investors, products, founders, and industry news. This API provides programmatic access to all core platform resources.

## Base URL

```
http://localhost:5000/api/v1

 ```

## Resources

| Resource | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Companies** | Browse, search, and manage startup/company profiles including funding timelines and products |
| **Investors** | Explore investor profiles, activity rankings, and investment portfolios |
| **Products** | Discover and retrieve product listings associated with companies |
| **News** | Access the latest and trending news articles in the tech ecosystem |
| **Search** | Full-text global search, autocomplete suggestions, and trending search terms |
| **Founders** | Look up founder profiles and their associated ventures |
| **Stats** | Retrieve high-level platform-wide statistics and metrics |

## Authentication

No authentication headers are currently configured at the collection level. If the API requires an API key or Bearer token, add it to the collection-level **Authorization** tab so all requests inherit it automatically.

## Versioning

All endpoints are versioned under `/api/v1/`. Ensure you are targeting the correct version when integrating with this API.

## Notes

- The API is currently running on `localhost:5000` — update the base URL to the appropriate staging or production host before deploying.
    
- Slug-based endpoints (e.g., `/companies/:slug`) use human-readable identifiers rather than numeric IDs.
    
- Use the **Search** endpoints for discovery before fetching specific resources by slug.

item.name: Companies

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of all companies on the platform. Supports optional filtering and pagination via query parameters.

## Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `page` | number | Optional. Page number for pagination. |
| `limit` | number | Optional. Number of results per page. |
| `filter` | string | Optional. Filter companies by a specific field or value. |

## Response

Returns a paginated array of company objects. Each object includes company metadata such as name, slug, category, stage, funding total, employee count, and location.

Fetches the detailed profile of a single company identified by its URL-friendly slug (e.g., `openai`). Returns company metadata including name, category, funding, employee count, stage, and location.

## Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `slug` | string | Required. The URL-friendly identifier of the company (e.g., `openai`, `stripe`). |

## Response

Returns a single company object with full profile details including:

- `id` — Unique identifier
    
- `name` — Company name
    
- `slug` — URL-friendly identifier
    
- `category` — Industry or product category
    
- `stage` — Funding stage (e.g., `SEED`, `SERIES_A`, `IPO`)
    
- `fundingTotal` — Total funding raised
    
- `employeeCount` — Number of employees
    
- `foundedYear` — Year the company was founded
    
- `hqCity` / `hqCountry` — Headquarters location

body.raw:
{
  "name": "OpenAI",
  "description": "Leading artificial intelligence research company building foundation models.",
  "category": "Foundation Models",
  "fundingTotal": 13000000000,
  "employeeCount": 5000,
  "foundedYear": 2015,
  "hqCity": "San Francisco",
  "hqCountry": "USA",
  "logoUrl": "https://logo.clearbit.com/openai.com",
  "website": "https://openai.com",
  "stage": "IPO",
  "valuation": 300000000000,
  "isUnicorn": true
}

request.description:
Returns a chronological list of funding rounds for a specific company identified by its slug. Each entry includes the round stage, amount raised, date, and investor details.

## Headers

| Key | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Required. Indicates the request format. |

## Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `slug` | string | Required. The URL-friendly identifier of the company (e.g., `openai`, `stripe`). |

## Response

Returns an array of funding round objects in chronological order. Each object includes:

- `stage` — Round stage (e.g., `SEED`, `SERIES_A`, `SERIES_B`)
    
- `amount` — Amount raised in the round
    
- `date` — Date the round was announced or closed
    
- `investors` — List of investors who participated in the round

Lists all products built or owned by a specific company, identified by its slug. Returns product names, slugs, descriptions, and related metadata.

## Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `slug` | string | Required. The URL-friendly identifier of the company (e.g., `openai`, `stripe`). |

## Response

Returns an array of product objects associated with the specified company. Each object includes:

- `name` — Product name
    
- `slug` — URL-friendly product identifier
    
- `description` — Short description of the product
    
- Additional metadata such as category, launch date, or status where available

Returns a list of companies currently gaining the most traction on the platform, ranked by engagement or activity metrics.

## Response

Returns an ordered array of trending company objects. Companies are ranked by platform engagement or activity signals. Each object includes standard company metadata such as:

- `name` — Company name
    
- `slug` — URL-friendly identifier
    
- `category` — Industry or product category
    
- `stage` — Current funding stage
    
- `fundingTotal` — Total funding raised
    
- Engagement or trending score where available

body.raw:
{
  "name": "OpenAI",
  "description": "AI company leading artificial intelligence research company building foundation models.",
  "category": "AI",
  "fundingTotal": 1000000,
  "employeeCount": 100,
  "foundedYear": 2015,
  "hqCity": "San Francisco",
  "hqCountry": "USA",
  "stage": "SEED"
}

request.description:
Creates a new company record on the platform. Requires `API_KEY` authentication. Returns the created company object with a generated `id` and `slug` on success (`201 Created`).

## Headers

| Key | Value | Description |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| `API_KEY` | `graphone_secret_2026` | Required. API key for authenticating the request. |

## Request Body

Send a JSON object with the following fields:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | string | Yes | The company's display name. |
| `description` | string | Yes | A short description of the company. |
| `category` | string | Yes | Industry or product category. |
| `fundingTotal` | number | Yes | Total funding raised (in USD or base currency). |
| `employeeCount` | number | Yes | Current number of employees. |
| `foundedYear` | number | Yes | Year the company was founded. |
| `hqCity` | string | Yes | City where the company is headquartered. |
| `hqCountry` | string | Yes | Country where the company is headquartered. |
| `stage` | string | Yes | Funding stage — one of `SEED`, `SERIES_A`, `SERIES_B`, ..., `IPO`. |

## Response

On success, returns `201 Created` with the newly created company object:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `id` | string | Auto-generated unique identifier. |
| `name` | string | Company name. |
| `slug` | string | Auto-generated URL-friendly identifier. |
| `category` | string | Industry or product category. |
| `stage` | string | Funding stage. |
| `fundingTotal` | string | Total funding raised. |
| `employeeCount` | number | Number of employees. |
| `foundedYear` | number | Year founded. |
| `hqCity` | string | Headquarters city. |
| `hqCountry` | string | Headquarters country. |
| `logoUrl` | null | Logo URL (null at creation). |
| `website` | null | Website URL (null at creation). |
| `valuation` | null | Company valuation (null at creation). |
| `isUnicorn` | boolean | Whether the company is valued at $1B+. |
| `createdAt` | string | ISO 8601 timestamp of when the record was created. |

item.description:
## Companies

Endpoints for browsing, retrieving, and managing company profiles on the GraphOne platform.

This folder covers:
- **Listing** all companies with optional filtering and pagination
- **Fetching** a specific company by its unique slug
- **Funding timeline** — a chronological history of funding rounds for a company
- **Products** — products built or owned by a specific company
- **Trending** — companies currently gaining traction on the platform
- **Creating** a new company record

Company resources are identified by a URL-friendly **slug** (e.g., `openai`) rather than a numeric ID.

item.name: Investors

request.description:
## GET Investors

Returns a paginated list of all investors on the platform. Supports optional filtering and pagination via query parameters.

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/investors`

---

### Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `page` | integer | No | Page number for pagination (default: 1). |
| `limit` | integer | No | Number of results per page (default: 10). |
| `type` | string | No | Filter investors by type (e.g., `vc`, `angel`, `corporate`). |
| `location` | string | No | Filter investors by geographic location. |

---

### Response

Returns a paginated JSON object containing a list of investor records.

**Success —** **`200 OK`**

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "string",
      "name": "string",
      "slug": "string",
      "type": "string",
      "location": "string",
      "totalInvestments": 0
    }
  ],
  "pagination": {
    "page": 1,
    "limit": 10,
    "total": 100
  }
}

 ```

**Error —** **`500 Internal Server Error`** — Unexpected server-side error.

## Get Investor Profile

Fetches the detailed profile of a specific investor identified by their URL-friendly slug (e.g., `sequoia-capital`). Returns investor metadata including name, type, location, and portfolio summary.

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/investors/:slug`

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `slug` | string | Yes | The URL-friendly unique identifier of the investor (e.g., `sequoia-capital`, `andreessen-horowitz`). |

---

### Response

Returns a detailed JSON object representing the investor's full profile.

**Success —** **`200 OK`**

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "id": "string",
    "name": "string",
    "slug": "string",
    "type": "string",
    "location": "string",
    "founded": "string",
    "website": "string",
    "description": "string",
    "portfolioSummary": {
      "totalInvestments": 0,
      "totalCompanies": 0,
      "activeInvestments": 0
    }
  }
}

 ```

**Error —** **`404 Not Found`** — No investor found matching the provided slug.  
**Error —** **`500 Internal Server Error`** — Unexpected server-side error.

## Get Most Active Investors

Returns a ranked list of investors ordered by deal volume or investment activity on the platform. Useful for identifying the most prolific investors within a given period or across all time.

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/investors/most-active`

---

### Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `limit` | integer | No | Number of top investors to return (default: 10). |
| `period` | string | No | Time period to filter activity (e.g., `30d`, `90d`, `1y`, `all`). |

---

### Response

Returns a ranked JSON array of the most active investors based on deal volume or investment frequency.

**Success —** **`200 OK`**

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": [
    {
      "rank": 1,
      "id": "string",
      "name": "string",
      "slug": "string",
      "type": "string",
      "location": "string",
      "totalDeals": 0,
      "totalInvested": "string"
    }
  ]
}

 ```

**Error —** **`500 Internal Server Error`** — Unexpected server-side error.

## Get Investor Investments

Lists the full investment portfolio of a specific investor identified by their slug. Returns details of each investment including company name, round stage, amount, and date.

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/investors/:slug/investments`

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `slug` | string | Yes | The URL-friendly unique identifier of the investor (e.g., `sequoia-capital`). |

### Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `page` | integer | No | Page number for pagination (default: 1). |
| `limit` | integer | No | Number of investment records per page (default: 10). |
| `stage` | string | No | Filter by funding round stage (e.g., `seed`, `series-a`, `series-b`). |

---

### Response

Returns a paginated JSON array of investment records associated with the specified investor.

**Success —** **`200 OK`**

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": [
    {
      "company": {
        "name": "string",
        "slug": "string"
      },
      "roundStage": "string",
      "amount": "string",
      "date": "string",
      "leadInvestor": true
    }
  ],
  "pagination": {
    "page": 1,
    "limit": 10,
    "total": 50
  }
}

 ```

**Error —** **`404 Not Found`** — No investor found matching the provided slug.  
**Error —** **`500 Internal Server Error`** — Unexpected server-side error.

item.description:
## Investors

Endpoints for exploring investor profiles and their investment activity on the GraphOne platform.

This folder covers:
- **Listing** all investors with optional filtering
- **Investor profile** — detailed information about a specific investor by slug
- **Most active investors** — a ranked list of investors by deal volume or activity
- **Investments** — the portfolio of investments made by a specific investor

Investor resources are identified by a URL-friendly **slug** (e.g., `sequoia-capital`).

item.name: Products

request.description:
## GET Products

Returns a paginated list of all products across the platform. Supports optional filtering and pagination via query parameters.

### Base URL

`http://localhost:5000/api/v1/products`

### Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `page` | integer | No | Page number for pagination (default: 1) |
| `limit` | integer | No | Number of results per page (default varies by server config) |
| `filter` | string | No | Optional filter criteria to narrow down product results |

### Response

Returns a paginated JSON object containing:

- A list of product records with their metadata
    
- Pagination details such as current page, total pages, and total count
    

### Example Response

``` json
{
  "data": [...],
  "page": 1,
  "limit": 10,
  "total": 100
}

 ```

## Get Product by Slug

Fetches the detailed profile of a specific product identified by its URL-friendly slug. Returns product metadata including name, description, category, and the company it belongs to.

### Base URL

`http://localhost:5000/api/v1/products/:slug`

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `slug` | string | Yes | The URL-friendly unique identifier of the product (e.g., `my-product-name`) |

### Response

Returns a JSON object containing the full product profile, including:

- `name` — Display name of the product
    
- `description` — Detailed description of the product
    
- `category` — The category the product belongs to
    
- `company` — The company associated with the product
    
- Additional product metadata
    

### Example Response

``` json
{
  "id": "abc123",
  "name": "Product Name",
  "slug": "product-name",
  "description": "A detailed description of the product.",
  "category": "SaaS",
  "company": {
    "id": "xyz789",
    "name": "Company Name"
  }
}

 ```

item.description:
## Products

Endpoints for discovering and retrieving product listings on the GraphOne platform.

This folder covers:
- **Listing** all products across the platform
- **Fetching** a specific product by its unique slug

Products are typically associated with a parent company and are identified by a URL-friendly **slug** (e.g., `d8syzxufya`).

item.name: News

request.description:
## GET News

Retrieves a paginated list of all news articles available on the platform.

### Base URL

`http://localhost:5000/api/v1/news`

### Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `topic` | string | Optional | Filter articles by a specific topic or category |
| `company` | string | Optional | Filter articles related to a specific company |
| `page` | integer | Optional | Page number for pagination (default: 1) |
| `limit` | integer | Optional | Number of articles per page (default varies by server config) |

### Response

Returns a paginated JSON object containing a list of news articles. Each article typically includes the title, summary, publication date, associated company or topic tags, and a link to the full article.

**Success —** **`200 OK`**

``` json
{
  "data": [...],
  "page": 1,
  "limit": 10,
  "total": 100
}

 ```

## Get Trending News

Retrieves news articles that are currently trending on the platform — ranked by engagement metrics such as views, shares, or user interactions.

### Base URL

`http://localhost:5000/api/v1/news/trending`

### Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `limit` | integer | Optional | Number of trending articles to return (default varies by server config) |
| `page` | integer | Optional | Page number for paginating through trending results |

### Response

Returns a JSON list of trending news articles ordered by engagement score. Each article typically includes the title, summary, publication date, engagement metrics, and associated tags or company references.

**Success —** **`200 OK`**

``` json
{
  "data": [...],
  "page": 1,
  "limit": 10,
  "total": 20
}

 ```

item.description:
## News

Endpoints for accessing news articles and editorial content within the GraphOne platform.

This folder covers:
- **Listing** all news articles, optionally filtered by topic or company
- **Trending news** — articles currently receiving the most engagement on the platform

item.name: Search

request.description:
Performs a full-text search across all resource types on the platform, including companies, investors, products, and founders.

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/search`

**Query Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `q` | string | Yes | The search term to query across all resource types |

**Response**

Returns categorized search results grouped by resource type (companies, investors, products, founders), each containing matching records relevant to the provided search term.

Returns real-time prefix-based autocomplete suggestions as the user types, enabling a search-as-you-type UI experience.

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/search/autocomplete`

**Query Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `q` | string | Yes | The prefix string typed by the user to generate autocomplete suggestions |

**Response**

Returns a list of autocomplete suggestions matching the provided prefix. Designed for low-latency, real-time use cases where suggestions update with each keystroke.

Returns the most popular search terms currently being used on the platform, useful for surfacing trending topics and popular queries to users.

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/search/trending`

**Query Parameters**

None required.

**Response**

Returns an ordered list of trending search terms based on current platform activity. Can be used to populate a "Trending" section in search UIs or to guide users toward popular content.

item.description:
## Search

Endpoints for discovering content across the GraphOne platform via full-text search and intelligent suggestions.

This folder covers:

- **Global search** — search across all resource types (companies, investors, products, founders) using a query string
    
- **Autocomplete** — real-time prefix-based suggestions as the user types
    
- **Trending searches** — the most popular search terms on the platform right now
    

All search endpoints accept a `q` query parameter for the search term.

item.name: Founders

request.description:
Fetches the biographical and professional details of a founder identified by their URL-friendly slug.

**Base URL:** `http://localhost:5000/founders/:slug`

> **Note:** This endpoint does **not** use the `/api/v1/` prefix. 
  

## Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `slug` | string | ✅ Yes | The URL-friendly identifier of the founder (e.g., `sam-altman`). |

## Response

Returns a JSON object containing the founder's biographical and professional details, including:

- **name** — Full name of the founder
    
- **bio** — Short biographical description
    
- **companies** — List of companies the founder is associated with
    
- **social_links** — Links to the founder's social media profiles (e.g., LinkedIn, Twitter/X)

item.description:
## Founders

Endpoints for retrieving founder profiles on the GraphOne platform.

This folder covers:
- **Founder profile** — detailed biographical and professional information about a specific founder

Founders are identified by a URL-friendly **slug** (e.g., `sam-altman`). Note that the founder endpoints use the path `/founders/:slug` (without the `/api/v1/` prefix).

item.name: Stats

request.description:
Returns high-level platform-wide metrics and aggregate statistics for the GraphOne platform.

**Base URL:** `http://localhost:5000/api/v1/stats`

## Path Parameters

None — this is a top-level stats endpoint with no path parameters.

## Response

Returns a JSON object containing aggregate counts and platform-wide metrics, including:

- **companies** — Total number of companies registered on the platform
    
- **investors** — Total number of investors registered on the platform
    
- **products** — Total number of products listed
    
- **news_articles** — Total number of news articles indexed
    
- **founders** — Total number of founders registered on the platform

item.description:
## Stats

Endpoints for retrieving aggregated platform-wide statistics and metrics.

This folder covers:
- **Platform stats** — high-level counts and metrics such as total companies, investors, products, and news articles indexed on the GraphOne platform
